title: [InstCombine] Extend folding of aggregate construction to cases when source aggregates are partially available

tests.source_program:
declare { ptr, i64 } @bar(i64)

define { ptr, i64 } @partial_predecessor_coverage(i1 %cond1, i1 %cond2, ptr %p1, ptr %p2) {
  br i1 %cond1, label %bbb1, label %bbb2

bbb1:                                             ; preds = %0
  br i1 %cond2, label %bbb3, label %bbb4

bbb3:                                             ; preds = %bbb1
  %call1 = call { ptr, i64 } @bar(i64 0)
  %val11 = extractvalue { ptr, i64 } %call1, 0
  %val12 = extractvalue { ptr, i64 } %call1, 1
  br label %exit

bbb4:                                             ; preds = %bbb1
  %val41 = load ptr, ptr %p1, align 8
  %val42 = load i64, ptr %p2, align 4
  br label %exit

bbb2:                                             ; preds = %0
  %val21 = load ptr, ptr %p1, align 8
  %val22 = load i64, ptr %p2, align 4
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %bbb2, %bbb4, %bbb3
  %val1 = phi ptr [ %val11, %bbb3 ], [ %val41, %bbb4 ], [ %val21, %bbb2 ]
  %val2 = phi i64 [ %val12, %bbb3 ], [ %val42, %bbb4 ], [ %val22, %bbb2 ]
  %tmp = insertvalue { ptr, i64 } poison, ptr %val1, 0
  %res = insertvalue { ptr, i64 } %tmp, i64 %val2, 1
  ret { ptr, i64 } %res
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare { ptr, i64 } @bar(i64)

define { ptr, i64 } @partial_predecessor_coverage(i1 %cond1, i1 %cond2, ptr %p1, ptr %p2) {
  br i1 %cond1, label %bbb1, label %bbb2

bbb1:                                             ; preds = %0
  br i1 %cond2, label %bbb3, label %bbb4

bbb3:                                             ; preds = %bbb1
  %call1 = call { ptr, i64 } @bar(i64 0)
  br label %exit

bbb4:                                             ; preds = %bbb1
  %val41 = load ptr, ptr %p1, align 8
  %val42 = load i64, ptr %p2, align 4
  %agg4 = insertvalue { ptr, i64 } poison, ptr %val41, 0
  %agg4.1 = insertvalue { ptr, i64 } %agg4, i64 %val42, 1
  br label %exit

bbb2:                                             ; preds = %0
  %val21 = load ptr, ptr %p1, align 8
  %val22 = load i64, ptr %p2, align 4
  %agg2 = insertvalue { ptr, i64 } poison, ptr %val21, 0
  %agg2.1 = insertvalue { ptr, i64 } %agg2, i64 %val22, 1
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %bbb2, %bbb4, %bbb3
  %res = phi { ptr, i64 } [ %call1, %bbb3 ], [ %agg4.1, %bbb4 ], [ %agg2.1, %bbb2 ]
  ret { ptr, i64 } %res
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions handling multiple predecessors but doesn't explicitly cover the case where only SOME predecessors have source aggregates while others need full construction. This tests the partial coverage scenario with 3 predecessors where only 1 has the original aggregate.

define { ptr, i64 } @partial_predecessor_coverage(i1 %cond1, i1 %cond2, ptr %p1, ptr %p2) {
  br i1 %cond1, label %bbb1, label %bbb2

bbb1:                                             ; preds = %0

bbb3:                                             ; preds = %bbb1
  %call1 = call { ptr, i64 } @bar(i64 0)

  %val12 = extractvalue { ptr, i64 } %call1, 1
  br label %exit

bbb4:                                             ; preds = %bbb1
  %val41 = load ptr, ptr %p1, align 8
  %val42 = load i64, ptr %p2, align 4
  br label %exit

bbb2:                                             ; preds = %0
  %val21 = load ptr, ptr %p1, align 8

declare { ptr, i64 } @bar(i64)

define { ptr, i64 } @partial_predecessor_coverage(i1 %cond1, i1 %cond2, ptr %p1, ptr %p2) {
  br i1 %cond1, label %bbb1, label %bbb2

bbb1:                                             ; preds = %0
  br i1 %cond2, label %bbb3, label %bbb4

bbb3:                                             ; preds = %bbb1
  %call1 = call { ptr, i64 } @bar(i64 0)
  br label %exit

bbb4:                                             ; preds = %bbb1
  %val41 = load ptr, ptr %p1, align 8
  %val42 = load i64, ptr %p2, align 4
  %1 = insertvalue { ptr, i64 } poison, ptr %val41, 0
  %2 = insertvalue { ptr, i64 } %1, i64 %val42, 1
  br label %exit

bbb2:                                             ; preds = %0
  %val21 = load ptr, ptr %p1, align 8
  %val22 = load i64, ptr %p2, align 4
  %3 = insertvalue { ptr, i64 } poison, ptr %val21, 0
  %4 = insertvalue { ptr, i64 } %3, i64 %val22, 1
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %bbb2, %bbb4, %bbb3
  %res.merged = phi { ptr, i64 } [ %call1, %bbb3 ], [ %2, %bbb4 ], [ %4, %bbb2 ]
  ret { ptr, i64 } %res.merged
}

tests.source_program:
declare { i32, i32 } @bar(i32)

define { i32, i32 } @test_predecessor_aggregate_construction(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %cond, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  %call1 = call { i32, i32 } @bar(i32 0)
  %val11 = extractvalue { i32, i32 } %call1, 0
  %val12 = extractvalue { i32, i32 } %call1, 1
  br label %exit

bb2:
  br label %exit

exit:
  %phi1 = phi i32 [ %val11, %bb1 ], [ %a, %bb2 ]
  %phi2 = phi i32 [ %val12, %bb1 ], [ %b, %bb2 ]
  %tmp = insertvalue { i32, i32 } poison, i32 %phi1, 0
  %res = insertvalue { i32, i32 } %tmp, i32 %phi2, 1
  ret { i32, i32 } %res
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare { i32, i32 } @bar(i32)

define { i32, i32 } @test_predecessor_aggregate_construction(i1 %cond, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %cond, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  %call1 = call { i32, i32 } @bar(i32 0)
  br label %exit

bb2:
  %agg.bb2 = insertvalue { i32, i32 } poison, i32 %a, 0
  %agg2.bb2 = insertvalue { i32, i32 } %agg.bb2, i32 %b, 1
  br label %exit

exit:
  %phi = phi { i32, i32 } [ %call1, %bb1 ], [ %agg2.bb2, %bb2 ]
  ret { i32, i32 } %phi
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: The summary mentions handling 'different aggregates from different predecessors' but does not explicitly describe constructing NEW aggregates in predecessors that have no source aggregate at all. This test exercises the code path where FoundSrcAgg is true (bb1 has extractvalue source) but bb2 needs aggregate construction from raw values.

tests.source_program:
define { i32, i32 } @test_elements_defined_before_usebb(i1 %cond, i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %agg_entry = insertvalue { i32, i32 } poison, i32 %x, 0
  %agg_entry2 = insertvalue { i32, i32 } %agg_entry, i32 %y, 1
  br i1 %cond, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  %val1 = extractvalue { i32, i32 } %agg_entry2, 0
  %val2 = extractvalue { i32, i32 } %agg_entry2, 1
  br label %exit

bb2:
  %val3 = extractvalue { i32, i32 } %agg_entry2, 0
  %val4 = extractvalue { i32, i32 } %agg_entry2, 1
  br label %exit

exit:
  %phi1 = phi i32 [ %val1, %bb1 ], [ %val3, %bb2 ]
  %phi2 = phi i32 [ %val2, %bb1 ], [ %val4, %bb2 ]
  %tmp = insertvalue { i32, i32 } poison, i32 %phi1, 0
  %res = insertvalue { i32, i32 } %tmp, i32 %phi2, 1
  ret { i32, i32 } %res
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define { i32, i32 } @test_elements_defined_before_usebb(i1 %cond, i32 %x, i32 %y) {
entry:
  %agg_entry = insertvalue { i32, i32 } poison, i32 %x, 0
  %agg_entry2 = insertvalue { i32, i32 } %agg_entry, i32 %y, 1
  br i1 %cond, label %bb1, label %bb2

bb1:
  br label %exit

bb2:
  br label %exit

exit:
  %phi = phi { i32, i32 } [ %agg_entry2, %bb1 ], [ %agg_entry2, %bb2 ]
  ret { i32, i32 } %phi
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: The summary mentions the EltDefinedInUseBB safety check but does not describe the positive case where elements are defined BEFORE UseBB (in entry), allowing the optimization to proceed. This tests that EltDefinedInUseBB remains false when values originate in predecessor blocks.
